title: The Little Old Table
content:
Creak, little wood thing, creak,
    When I touch you with elbow or knee;
    That is the way you speak
    Of one who gave you to me!

    You, little table, she brought -
    Brought me with her own hand,
    As she looked at me with a thought
    That I did not understand.

    - Whoever owns it anon,
    And hears it, will never know
    What a history hangs upon
    This creak from long ago.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Hardy, titled "The Little Old Table", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
